summary: Google follows almost everything you do online, including what you search for, where you go, and who you talk to. They use this huge pile of info to show you ads and build a detailed profile of your life.
risk_reason: They track your activity on millions of other websites and apps, not just their own.
data_collection: Your name, phone number, and payment info
Every search term and video you watch
Your exact location and where you live or work
The content of your emails, photos, and documents
Apps you use and how you use your phone
third_party_sharing: Companies that help them process info
Government or police if they ask for it legally
Your boss or school if you use their account
tracking_cookies: Tracks you across millions of non-Google websites
Identifies your specific computer or phone
Remembers what you do across different sessions
user_rights: You can see and delete your search history
You can download a copy of your info
You can tell them to stop showing personalized ads
You can delete your entire account
suspicious_clauses: They collect info about you from 'public sources' like newspapers or the web
They track you on other apps even if you use 'Incognito' mode
They can listen to your saved audio to 'improve' their systems
key_risks: Google creates a permanent, detailed diary of your private life that is very hard to fully escape if you use the internet.
